LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CURRENT TV, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION, CURRENT MEDIA, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION & AL GORE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF TAP HAS NOT PLED ANY FACTS TO ALLEGE DAMAGES FROM DEFENDANTS' PURPORTED ACTIONS. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PLED ANY FACTS TO ALLEGE THE PERSONAL LIABILITY OF MR. GORE FOR PLAINTIFFS' ALLEGED DEALINGS WITH DEFENDANTS CURRENT TV AND CURRENT MEDIA. AS PLED, MR. TERENZIO ALLEGES THAT HE INTENDED TO BROKER THE SALE OF A BUSINESS OPPORTUNITY THAT IS WITHIN THE PURVIEW OF BUS. & PROF. CODE 10130, 10131(A). (COMPLAINT, 16, 17, 25.) PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT ALLEGED ANY FACTS TO SHOW THAT MR. BLUM WAS DEFENDANTS' AGENT. LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, FOR PLAINTIFFS TO DISTINGUISH THEIR PROPOSED INVOLVEMENT IN THE SALE OF CURRENT TV AND TO ALLEGE ESTOPPEL BASED ON THEORIES OF OSTENSIBLE AUTHORITY AND RATIFICATION. FOR THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, UNJUST ENRICHMENT IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION. (MELCHIOR V. NEW LINE PRODUCTIONS, INC. (2003) 106 CAL.APP.4TH 779, 793.) FOR THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT ALLEGED THAT DEFENDANTS' CONSENT OR THAT DEFENDANTS REQUESTED MR. TERENZIO'S SERVICES, BUT RATHER THAT MR. TERENZIO PRESENTED HIS UNSOLICITED PROPOSAL TO DEFENDANTS. (COMPLAINT, 12, 13.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. MINI MINUTES:

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTs CURRENT TV, LLC and CURRENT MEDIA, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents is GRANTED IN PART. Defendants' requests for documents reflecting the terms of Plaintiff Terenzio's employment related to the Judge Joe Brown, Swift Justice, Fox Cable Networks CCTV engagement, HRTV, and the Fox dating show seek information relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action. Plaintiff's complaint alleges that "Terenzio's presentation to Blum was based on the mutual understanding that Terenzio would be compensated both as a finder and for his media-related services, if Current consummated its sale to Al Jazeera" (Complaint Par. 22) and that, "Terenzio proposed that in addition to his services as a finder, he would consult and manage the public relations and creative aspects of the sale (Complaint Par. 23)." Moreover, Plaintiff testified about the terms of the contracts at his deposition. Under these circumstances, the state's interest in facilitating the ascertainment of truth in connection with legal proceedings is substantial enough to compel disclosure of these particular contracts, but not documents reflecting other employment engagements dating back to 1991 that Defendants also seek. Plaintiff shall produce copies of any executed contracts related to the Judge Joe Brown, Swift Justice, CCTV, HRTV, and Fox dating show engagements, and may designate the contracts as "CONFIDENTIAL - OUTSIDE COUNSEL'S EYES ONLY" pursuant to a stipulated protective order. Request for sanctions is DENIED.

A proposed Order was submitted by messenger along with a stipulated protective order. Both orders were signed. Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CURRENT TV, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION, CURRENT MEDIA, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION & AL GORE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the first and third causes of action. Sustained, without leave to amend as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff TAP has not pled any facts to allege damages from Defendants' purported actions. Plaintiffs have not pled any facts to allege the personal liability of Mr. Gore for Plaintiffs' alleged dealings with Defendants Current TV and Current Media. As pled, Mr. Terenzio alleges that he intended to broker the sale of a business opportunity that is within the purview of Bus. & Prof. Code 10130, 10131(a). (Complaint, 16, 17, 25.) Plaintiffs have not alleged any facts to show that Mr. Blum was Defendants' agent. Leave to amend is granted, if they can do so in good faith, for Plaintiffs to distinguish their proposed involvement in the sale of Current TV and to allege estoppel based on theories of ostensible authority and ratification. For the second cause of action, unjust enrichment is not a cause of action. (Melchior v. New Line Productions, Inc. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 779, 793.) For the third cause of action, Plaintiffs have not alleged that Defendants' consent or that Defendants requested Mr. Terenzio's services, but rather that Mr. Terenzio presented his unsolicited proposal to Defendants. (Complaint, 12, 13.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
